title: Scientific Agent Skills
oneLiner: Open-source ready-to-use agent skills for research, science, engineering, analysis, finance, and writing.
summary: Scientific Agent Skills is an MIT-licensed collection of reusable skills for research and technical work, aimed at agents that need more structured procedures than a single prompt can provide.
whyItMatters: Scientific Agent Skills matters because research and analysis tasks need repeatable methods. A skill pack can encode reusable procedures for literature review, engineering analysis, finance work, and writing support without forcing the agent to invent the workflow every time.
bestFor: Teams building agents for scientific, finance, engineering, or writing tasks
Developers studying how to package domain procedures as skills
notFor: Users who want a finished research assistant with hosted UI
Teams that need validated domain outputs without human review
seoTitle: Scientific Agent Skills: Open-source skills for research agents
seoDescription: Scientific Agent Skills profile: what the open-source skill pack is, when to use it, official links, command line, and OpenAgent structured data.
shareDescription: Reusable open-source skills for research, science, engineering, analysis, finance, and writing agents.

coreStrengths.title: Domain-oriented skill collection
coreStrengths.description: The repository focuses on research, science, engineering, analysis, finance, and writing workflows.
coreStrengths.whyItMatters: Domain skills can give agents more useful procedures than generic prompting.

coreStrengths.title: Reusable workflow packaging
coreStrengths.description: Skills can be inspected, reused, and adapted for specific agent systems.
coreStrengths.whyItMatters: Repeatability is especially important in research and analysis work.

coreStrengths.title: Good source for skill taxonomy ideas
coreStrengths.description: The project helps clarify how broad skill collections might be organized across domains.
coreStrengths.whyItMatters: OpenAgent can use this kind of project to distinguish skills from agents, plugins, and tools.

useCaseNotes.title: Research agent procedures
useCaseNotes.description: Use it as a starting point for agents that need repeatable research and analysis steps.

useCaseNotes.title: Domain workflow prototyping
useCaseNotes.description: Adapt skill patterns for engineering, finance, scientific analysis, or writing workflows.

useCaseNotes.title: Skill library design
useCaseNotes.description: Study how domain-specific skills are named, grouped, and documented.

compareNotes.title: Choose Scientific Agent Skills for domain-heavy workflows
compareNotes.summary: General packs are useful for broad automation. Scientific Agent Skills is more relevant when the agent must follow research or analysis procedures.

commandLine.label: Clone the skills repository
commandLine.description: Start from the official repository before adapting any skill to your agent runtime.

seoArticle.intro: Scientific Agent Skills is useful because many research and analysis workflows need more than a broad instruction. They need repeatable procedures that an agent can follow, inspect, and refine.
seoArticle.whatItIs: Scientific Agent Skills is an MIT-licensed repository of ready-to-use agent skills for research, science, engineering, analysis, finance, and writing. It is a skill collection, not a single agent app.
seoArticle.whyItMatters: Domain tasks are where generic agents often become vague. A skill pack can narrow the workflow: what to inspect, what to calculate, what to compare, and how to produce a useful output.
seoArticle.howItWorks: Start by reviewing the repository structure, then pick one narrow skill and test it with a real task. Treat output as assisted work that still needs human review, especially in scientific, financial, or engineering contexts.

useCases.title: Literature and research workflows
useCases.description: Use skill patterns to make review, synthesis, and note-taking tasks more repeatable.

useCases.title: Technical analysis workflows
useCases.description: Adapt skills for engineering or data analysis tasks where a defined procedure matters.

useCases.title: Agent skill benchmarking
useCases.description: Compare domain-specific skills against general-purpose agent prompts.

alternatives.title: Use Hugging Face Skills for model-hub workflows
alternatives.summary: Hugging Face Skills is stronger near the model ecosystem. Scientific Agent Skills is stronger for research and domain procedures.

faq.question: Is Scientific Agent Skills open source?
faq.answer: The GitHub repository is listed with an MIT license.

faq.question: Is this a research agent?
faq.answer: No. It is a collection of skills that can be used by agents, not a full hosted assistant.

faq.question: Should outputs be trusted without review?
faq.answer: No. Research, finance, science, and engineering outputs should always be reviewed by a qualified human.

thumbnailBrief.visualMotif: stacked research notebooks and modular command blocks
thumbnailBrief.backgroundStyle: clean editorial surface with subtle graph and table motifs
thumbnailBrief.subtitle: Reusable skills for research agents
